title: Agalinis filicaulis
taxonomy: Agalinis filicaulis is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It belongs to the genus Agalinis, which is part of the order Lamiales. The plant is also known as False Foxglove or False Foxglove False Foxglove or False Foxglove. Agalinis filicaulis is native to North America. This plant is classified as a hemiparasitic perennial.

intro: Agalinis filicaulis, commonly known as green false dragonhead, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Orobanchaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Agalinis filicaulis include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Agalinis filicaulis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not widely documented in modern medical literature.
